description: This file contains Balanda conjunctions and connectors. Conjunctions and connectors link two or more clauses to express contrast, cause, result, conclusion, alternative, or accompaniment. Connectors covered: kee (but), andiso (because), wamina (therefore), ana (so), wala (or), ta (with).

kee.notes: Signals an unexpected or opposing relationship between two clauses. Often paired with gba (negation) in the second clause.

andiso.notes: Introduces the cause or reason for the main clause. Main clause comes first; andiso opens the reason clause.

wamina.notes: Draws a logical conclusion from the preceding clause. More formal than ana.

ana.notes: Introduces the natural result of the preceding event. Similar to wamina but more narrative and less formal.

wala.notes: Presents two options of which one is chosen or possible.

ta.notes: Marks accompaniment, instrument, or shared participation.

input: Ni timande kee ni ja nja gba.
output: She works but she does not eat.

input: Ne leyai agbi maraya kee ne mamve womolo madhu gba.
output: I will go to school but I cannot travel far.

input: Ni taakeye ti gbiya kee gbiya taadhii gba.
output: He called the chief but the chief did not come.

input: Kawihi taadheke fökpaa kee ndi taawunjo gba.
output: The children read the book but did not agree.

input: Dhuu taalo nja kee gbiya taaja nja gba.
output: We cooked food but the chief did not eat.

input: Ni merego diktor kee ni konu ni gba.
output: She loves the doctor but does not help him.

input: Ndi taawomolo agbi negu kee ndi taadele gba.
output: They traveled to the market but did not come back.

input: Ne ledheke fökpaa kee ne leferege ay gba.
output: I will read the book but I will not explain it.

input: Ŋo nonve mande kee ŋo mamve konu gba.
output: You can work but you cannot help.

input: Wihi taandiri agbi maraya kee ni taadheke gba.
output: The child went to school but did not read.

input: Ni taamaa wiki kee ni taaze ay zi bihiwutdu gba.
output: He made porridge but did not give it to anyone.

input: Dhuu kawunjo ki kee dhuu katuna gba.
output: We agreed but we did not start.

input: Ni taalunde garra kee ni kamamve veleso gba.
output: She used the phone but could not speak.

input: Ne taayai a le serre kee ne taanzü ngo gba.
output: I went to the river but did not drink water.

input: Ndi lekonu kawihi kee ndi ledhii agbi tiyi gba.
output: They will help the children but will not come to the house.

input: Ne tija no andiso nja no muru kafa.
output: I eat because food is on the table.

input: Ni titeli no andiso ni taamande walita.
output: She sleeps because she worked all day.

input: Dhuu kakeye ti diktor no andiso wihi kadhii a ba.
output: We called the doctor because the child came home.

input: Ni tiwomolo no andiso ni merego negu.
output: He travels because he loves the market.

input: Ndi kadheke fökpaa no andiso diktor kaferege ay ki.
output: They read the book because the doctor explained it.

input: Ne lekonu ŋo andiso ŋo kakonu ne ki.
output: I will help you because you helped me.

input: Gbiya kadhii no andiso kaküma kawunjo ki.
output: The chief came because the heroes agreed.

input: Ni mamve mande gba andiso ni kateli gba.
output: She cannot work because she did not sleep.

input: Dhuu timaa nja no andiso kanvewilli no agbi tiyi.
output: We make food because the guests are at the house.

input: Ni leyai agbi maraya andiso ni merego adheke.
output: He will go to school because he loves to read.

input: Ne kawunjo no andiso dhako na kaferege fökpaa ki.
output: I agreed because the man explained the book.

input: Ni lunde garra no andiso wanaa'u kakeye to'o ki.
output: She uses the phone because the brother called.

input: Ndi mamve düŋü gba andiso turunvili kono willa.
output: They cannot remain because the car is not here.

input: Dhuu lekuta andiso ŋo kaze fökpaa zi dhuu ki.
output: We will commence because you gave us the book.

input: Diktor terei no andiso wihi kadhii gba.
output: The doctor thinks because the child did not come.

input: Ni taamande tivo, wamina ni leteli.
output: She worked hard; therefore she will sleep.

input: Kawilii kadheke fökpaa ki, wamina ndi kaŋiyi ki.
output: The girls read the book; therefore they knew.

input: Dhuu taakonu kanjö, wamina ndi taafoyi.
output: We helped the orphans; therefore they appreciated.

input: Ni kakeye ti niki ŋana ki, wamina ni leviya gba.
output: He called the woman; therefore she will not decline.

input: Ne kateri ay ki, wamina ne letuna.
output: I thought about it; therefore I will start.

input: Ndi taandiri madhu, wamina ndi lewoti.
output: They went far; therefore they will rest.

input: Vedi no muru kafa, wamina ay ledho dhiki.
output: The charcoal is on the table; therefore it will be black.

input: Ni taaferege nde, wamina dhuu taadhigbi.
output: She showed the work; therefore we understood.

input: Ne taaze liki zi vakuru ne, wamina ni no kosere songo.
output: I gave honey to my friend; therefore he is beside the container.

input: Ŋo kalunde kese ki, wamina ŋo leni tirri.
output: You used the bike; therefore you will carry the load.

input: Kanako kalu ki, wamina dhuu lekuta süüno.
output: The people arrived; therefore we will commence soon.

input: Ndi kanangele ki, wamina alegili ndi.
output: They ran away; therefore they will be missed.

input: Ni merego amoliki, wamina ni ledhe kavakure'u.
output: She loves playing; therefore she will meet her friends.

input: Dhuu mamve dülü gba, wamina dhuu ledhota kono.
output: We cannot dance; therefore we will sit here.

input: Gavo'o taamodhi, wamina ne ledelesiggi.
output: The grandfather laughed; therefore I will respond.

input: Diktor taadhii, ana dhuu taaveleso ta ni.
output: The doctor came, so we spoke with him.

input: Dha taalo nja ana ne taaja gba.
output: You cooked food, so I didn't eat.

input: Ne taadhe kümü, ana ne taasi amande.
output: I found wealth, so I stopped working.

input: Ndi taayükü, ana goho aja nja tiyaya tindi willa.
output: They got fat, so they don't need to eat recklessly.

input: Ni taamgba mva, ana ni no gbi ngirimi.
output: He stole the knife, so he is in trouble.

input: Dhuu taatuna yerre, ana kanako taasoho.
output: We started the comedy, so the people cheered.

input: Ni taayoro siggi zi ni, ana ni taasadi yi wei.
output: She sent a message to him, so he sold the thing.

input: Bura no muru yabi nguu, ana ndi taamoko ni.
output: The cat is on the tree branch, so they chased it.

input: Ne taakonu kanvemande, ana ndi merego ne.
output: I helped the workers, so they love me.

input: Ndi taadhekenjo, ana dhuu leyai kulo.
output: They pleaded, so we will proceed.

input: Ni taandugu, ana ni taakpokoro.
output: He exercised, so he became fit.

input: Dhuu taawofoso, ana dhuu taawunjo.
output: We discussed, so we agreed.

input: Ni taawawiwo, ana wö ni taasoi.
output: She sweated, so she felt tired.

input: Siriga taaveleso ana ndi taasüsü.
output: The pioneer spoke, so everyone listened.

input: Ne taaboroko turunvili, ana ne taawirindi ay.
output: I crashed the car, so I repaired it.

input: Ni leyai agbi maraya wala ni lemande.
output: He will go to school or he will work.

input: Ni lelo nja wala ni leyai agbi negu.
output: She will cook food or she will go to the market.

input: Ne lekeye ti diktor wala ne leyai bi diktor.
output: I will call the doctor or I will go to him.

input: Ndi nonve mande wala ndi nonve womolo.
output: They can work or they can travel.

input: Ŋo ledheke fökpaa wala ŋo leveleso zi gbiya.
output: You will read the book or you will speak to the chief.

input: Dhuu lekonu kawihi wala dhuu lekeye ti diktor.
output: We will help the children or we will call the doctor.

input: Ni kaze no nja wala ngo zi wihi.
output: He gave food or water to the child.

input: Ni kanonmve mande wala ni kanonmve womolo.
output: She would work or she would travel.

input: Ne lemaa nja wala ne leyai va moliki.
output: I will make food or I will go to play.

input: Kawihi ledheke wala ndi leteli.
output: The children will read or they will sleep.

input: Ŋo nonve keye ti ne wala ŋo nonve dhii agbi tiyi.
output: You can call me or you can come to the house.

input: Ni lewunjo wala ni leferege.
output: He will agree or he will explain.

input: Dhuu lewomolo ta turunvili wala dhuu leyai le serre.
output: We will travel by car or we will go to the river.

input: Ni lemerego wihi wala ni lesogo ni.
output: She will love the child or she will hate him.

input: Ndi no tiyeti wala ndi no tikoloti.
output: Are they fighting or are they cuddling?

input: Ni yai agbi negu ta kawihi.
output: She goes to the market with the children.

input: Ne nzü ngo ta diktor.
output: I drink water with the doctor.

input: Ni taawomolo le serre ta gbiya.
output: He traveled to the river with the chief.

input: Dhuu taalo nja ta kawihi kosere tiyi.
output: We cooked food with the children beside the house.

input: Ni leyai agbi maraya ta bura ngandi ni.
output: She will go to school with her cat.

input: Diktor kadhii no ta fökpaa.
output: The doctor came with the book.

input: Ne lemande ta ŋo lüve.
output: I will work with you tomorrow.

input: Ndi taawunjo ta gbiya.
output: They agreed with the chief.

input: Ni ja nja ta niki ngando'o muru kafa.
output: He eats food with his wife at the table.

input: Ni taaveleso ta dhako ngando'o agbi tiyi.
output: She spoke with her husband at the house.

input: Dhuu lewomolo ta turunvili agbi negu.
output: We will travel with the car to the market.

input: Wihi tidheke fökpaa ta nverugu.
output: The child reads the book with the teacher.

input: Ne taakeye ti ŋo ta garra.
output: I called you with the phone.

input: Ni taanjuku nja ta gbiya süüta danga.
output: He prepared food with the chief near the kitchen.

input: Ni kanonmve yai ta ne wala ni kanonmve teli agbi tiyi.
output: She would go with me or stay at home.

input: kee
output: kee — but (contrast / concession). Signals an unexpected or opposing relationship between two clauses. Often paired with gba (negation) in the second clause. Example: Ni timande kee ni ja nja gba. — She works but she does not eat.

input: andiso
output: andiso — because (cause / reason). Introduces the cause or reason for the main clause. Main clause comes first; andiso opens the reason clause. Example: Ne tija no andiso nja no muru kafa. — I eat because food is on the table.

input: wamina
output: wamina — therefore (logical conclusion). Draws a logical conclusion from the preceding clause. Structure: [Premise]; wamina [Conclusion]. Example: Ni taamande tivo, wamina ni leteli. — She worked hard; therefore she will sleep.

input: ana
output: ana — so (result / consequence). Introduces the natural result of the preceding event. Similar to wamina but more narrative and less formal. Example: Diktor taadhii, ana dhuu taaveleso ta ni. — The doctor came, so we spoke with him.

input: wala
output: wala — or (alternative / choice). Presents two options of which one is chosen or possible. Structure: [Option A] wala [Option B]. Example: Ni leyai agbi maraya wala ni lemande. — He will go to school or he will work.

input: ta
output: ta — with (association / accompaniment). Marks accompaniment, instrument, or shared participation. Structure: Verb phrase + ta + companion/instrument. Example: Ni yai agbi negu ta kawihi. — She goes to the market with the children.
